tagline: Film room intelligence at machine speed.

round_of_64.reasoning: Auburn's AdjEM of 34.7 dwarfs Alabama State's -8.4. A 43-point efficiency gap is insurmountable — Auburn's interior presence and elite defense smother any 16-seed. Coaching and roster depth aren't even factors here.

Louisville edges Creighton in adjusted defense (95.9 vs 98.7) and overall efficiency margin (22.0 vs 19.7). In a coin-flip 8-9 game, the team that guards better usually survives. Louisville's slight defensive edge is the difference.

Michigan holds a 6.4-point AdjEM edge (21.8 vs 15.4) with superior offense and defense. Classic 5-12 upset zone, but UC San Diego's slower tempo won't generate enough possessions to close the talent gap.
Texas A&M's defensive efficiency (94.2) is a problem Yale's Ivy League offense (AdjO 114.0) can't overcome. Nearly 12-point AdjEM gap. The physicality mismatch at the 4-13 line is too steep.

Ole Miss and North Carolina are nearly identical offensively (AdjO 119.3 each), but Ole Miss has the defensive edge (97.0 vs 99.8). In a 6-11 matchup between SEC teams, the better defensive team at a controlled tempo wins.
Iowa State's 20-point AdjEM advantage (26.0 vs 5.7) is overwhelming. Elite defense (AdjD 93.3) will strangle Lipscomb's limited offense. 3-seeds beat 14-seeds at an 85%+ clip historically.

Marquette's 2.5-point AdjEM edge is modest, but their defensive efficiency (97.0) better contains New Mexico's high-tempo attack (72.9 possessions). Big East-tested defense handles Mountain West pace.
Michigan State's 24.4-point AdjEM gap (24.9 vs 0.5) is decisive. Elite defense (AdjD 92.3) against a team barely at replacement level. Tom Izzo's tournament pedigree adds coaching dominance.
Duke's AdjEM of 38.5 is the highest in the tournament field. A 46-point efficiency gap over Mount St. Mary's (-7.5) is the largest mismatch in the bracket. Duke's AdjO of 130.6 is generational.

Baylor's superior AdjEM (21.0 vs 19.2) comes from elite offense (AdjO 121.3 vs 118.4). Both play similar tempos but Baylor generates better looks. Scott Drew's tournament track record gives coaching edge.

Oregon's 7.2-point AdjEM edge (20.2 vs 13.0) with superior offense and defense makes this a clear 5-seed win. Liberty's slower tempo (65.7) limits possessions, but Oregon's efficiency advantage holds up.
Arizona's AdjEM of 27.5 towers over Akron's 6.6 — a 21-point gap. Arizona's explosive offense (AdjO 124.9) at a fast tempo (70.3) will overwhelm Akron's porous defense (AdjD 106.9).
BYU's elite offense (AdjO 126.8) and 7.3-point AdjEM edge (26.6 vs 19.3) are decisive. VCU has better defensive efficiency (96.7 vs 100.2), but BYU's scoring volume overwhelms at this seed line.
Wisconsin's 23.5-point AdjEM advantage (26.4 vs 2.9) is comprehensive — better offense, much better defense. Wisconsin's deliberate tempo (67.9) denies Montana any fast-break opportunities.

Saint Mary's defensive efficiency (AdjD 93.0) is elite — 8 points better than Vanderbilt's (101.1). Playing at the slowest tempo in the field (61.9) limits Vanderbilt's transition opportunities and turns this into a half-court grinder Saint Mary's dominates.
Alabama's AdjEM of 31.1 crushes Robert Morris's 2.1. Alabama plays the fastest tempo in the field (75.1), generating more possessions where their offensive firepower (AdjO 127.7) buries a 15-seed.
Houston's AdjD of 87.3 is the best in the country — 18.5 points better than SIU Edwardsville's offense can produce. A 42-point AdjEM gap (37.3 vs -4.9) is annihilation territory.

Gonzaga's AdjEM of 28.1 is absurdly high for an 8-seed — 9.3 points better than Georgia (18.8). Gonzaga's explosive offense (AdjO 124.8) at a fast tempo (70.5) creates a pace Georgia can't match. This is a misseeded team.

Clemson's 7.6-point AdjEM edge (21.8 vs 14.2) with better defense (AdjD 95.5 vs 99.7) handles McNeese's scoring. Both play slow tempos (64-66 range), which favors the more disciplined Clemson defense.
Purdue's elite offense (AdjO 125.6) and 16.6-point AdjEM gap (26.2 vs 9.6) make this straightforward. High Point's porous defense (AdjD 108.2) can't contain Purdue's scoring machine.

Illinois holds a 6.1-point AdjEM edge (24.0 vs 17.9) driven by superior offense (AdjO 122.5 vs 116.8). Both play similar tempos and have comparable defense, but Illinois's scoring volume at Big Ten pace creates separation.

Kentucky's AdjEM of 24.9 holds an 18.6-point advantage over Troy (6.3). Kentucky's offense (AdjO 123.4) at a faster tempo (70.9 vs 66.4) generates more possessions and exploits Troy's average defense.

UCLA's defensive efficiency (AdjD 94.3) is 11 points better than Utah State's (105.4). Despite Utah State's strong offense (AdjO 121.3), UCLA's 7.8-point AdjEM edge and elite half-court defense control this game.

Tennessee's AdjD of 91.9 is the second-best in the country. Combined with a 26.3-point AdjEM gap (28.3 vs 2.0), Wofford has no path to scoring enough points. Tennessee's slow, grinding tempo (63.9) eliminates variance.

Florida's 36.5-point AdjEM advantage (33.6 vs -2.9) is comprehensive. Elite offense (AdjO 127.8) combined with strong defense (AdjD 94.2) at a fast tempo — Norfolk State simply cannot compete at this level.

UConn's AdjEM edge (19.6 vs 15.9) comes from superior offense (AdjO 120.8 vs 116.8). UConn plays slower (64.1 tempo) which limits possessions and favors their offensive efficiency advantage in a half-court game.

Colorado State is the higher-rated team despite being the 12-seed — AdjEM 18.4 vs Memphis's 12.8. Better offense (AdjO 117.4 vs 112.1), better defense (AdjD 99.0 vs 99.3). The numbers don't lie: this 5-seed is overseeded.
Maryland's AdjEM of 26.5 crushes Grand Canyon's 7.7 with an 18.8-point gap. Maryland's elite defense (AdjD 92.1) will suffocate Grand Canyon's modest offense (AdjO 107.9).

Missouri's 9.3-point AdjEM edge (24.7 vs 15.4) and elite offense (AdjO 125.9) overwhelm Drake's approach. Drake's glacial tempo (59.1) limits possessions, but Missouri's offensive firepower generates enough separation even in fewer possessions.
Texas Tech's 22.8-point AdjEM gap (28.5 vs 5.7) with elite offense (AdjO 126.1) makes this a blowout. UNC Wilmington's defense (AdjD 106.7) can't contain this level of offensive firepower.
Kansas holds a 4.1-point AdjEM edge (22.3 vs 18.2) with better defense (AdjD 93.7 vs 95.5). Bill Self's tournament experience gives Kansas the coaching edge in a tight 7-10 matchup at similar tempos.
St. John's boasts the best defense in the bracket by AdjD (90.0) and a 19.9-point AdjEM gap (22.9 vs 3.0). Omaha's offense (AdjO 112.1) drops dramatically against this defensive wall.

round_of_32.reasoning: Auburn's AdjEM of 34.7 is 12.7 points higher than Louisville's 22.0. Auburn's defense (AdjD 93.0) contains Louisville's offense while Auburn's elite attack (AdjO 127.7) overwhelms Louisville's defensive profile.

Comparable AdjEM (21.8 each) but Texas A&M has the defensive edge (AdjD 94.2 vs 93.9) and marginally better offense. In a tight 4-5 matchup, Texas A&M's physicality and SEC-tested defense give them the slight advantage.

Iowa State's AdjEM (26.0) is 3.8 points above Ole Miss (22.2), driven by superior defense (AdjD 93.3 vs 97.0). Iowa State's defensive system creates turnovers and limits quality looks — exactly the style that neutralizes Ole Miss's offense.

Michigan State's AdjEM (24.9) towers over Marquette's (19.4) with a 5.5-point gap driven by elite defense (AdjD 92.3 vs 97.0). Tom Izzo's tournament track record in second-round games adds a decisive coaching edge.

Duke's AdjEM of 38.5 is 17.5 points above Baylor's 21.0. Duke's generational offense (AdjO 130.6) scores on any defense, and Duke's own defense (AdjD 92.0) contains Baylor's efficient but less explosive attack.

Arizona's 7.3-point AdjEM advantage (27.5 vs 20.2) is decisive in a Pac-12 rivalry matchup. Arizona's offense (AdjO 124.9) at a fast tempo (70.3) generates more scoring opportunities than Oregon's defense can contain.

Nearly identical AdjEM (BYU 26.6 vs Wisconsin 26.4) but Wisconsin's deliberate tempo (67.9) limits BYU's explosive offense. Wisconsin's superior defense (AdjD 96.8 vs 100.2) controls the game in a half-court slugfest.

Alabama's AdjEM (31.1) overwhelms Saint Mary's (21.2) despite Saint Mary's elite defense (AdjD 93.0). Alabama's frantic pace (75.1 tempo vs 61.9) forces Saint Mary's out of their comfort zone and generates enough possessions to overcome defensive resistance.

Houston vs Gonzaga is defense vs offense — Houston's AdjD (87.3) is the best in the country. Despite Gonzaga's elite offense (AdjO 124.8), Houston's 9.2-point AdjEM edge (37.3 vs 28.1) and suffocating defense prevail at Houston's slow tempo.

Purdue's AdjEM (26.2) is 4.4 points above Clemson's (21.8). Purdue's offensive efficiency (AdjO 125.6 vs 117.3) creates a scoring gap Clemson's defense cannot close. Both play slow, methodical tempos, which favors the better half-court team.

Close matchup (AdjEM 24.9 vs 24.0) but Kentucky's edge comes from slightly better offense (AdjO 123.4 vs 122.5) at a marginally faster pace. Kentucky's roster talent and traditional pedigree give them the edge in a toss-up.

Tennessee's AdjEM (28.3) beats UCLA's (23.6) by 4.7 points, driven by elite defense (AdjD 91.9 vs 94.3). At Tennessee's deliberate tempo (63.9), possessions are limited and defensive efficiency becomes the deciding factor.

Florida's AdjEM (33.6) is 14 points above UConn's (19.6). Florida's offense (AdjO 127.8) and defense (AdjD 94.2) are both dramatically better. Even UConn's championship program pedigree can't overcome this statistical mismatch.

Maryland's AdjEM (26.5) is 8.1 points above Colorado State's (18.4). Maryland's elite defense (AdjD 92.1) shuts down Colorado State's offense. Despite Colorado State's first-round upset, the talent gap becomes clear against a true 4-seed.

Texas Tech's AdjEM (28.5) is 3.8 points above Missouri's (24.7). Texas Tech's defense and offense are both marginally better. Missouri's high-powered offense (AdjO 125.9) keeps it competitive, but Tech's balanced profile prevails.

St. John's AdjD of 90.0 is the best in the bracket, and their AdjEM edge (22.9 vs 22.3) is slight but supported by that defensive dominance. Kansas's offense (AdjO 116.0) struggles against this elite defensive wall.

sweet_16.reasoning: Auburn's AdjEM (34.7) is 12.9 points higher than Texas A&M's (21.8). Both are SEC teams, but Auburn's offensive firepower (AdjO 127.7 vs 116.0) and defensive edge create an insurmountable gap at the Sweet 16 stage.

Razor-thin AdjEM edge for Iowa State (26.0 vs 24.9). Iowa State's defense (AdjD 93.3 vs 92.3) is slightly worse, but their offense (AdjO 119.3 vs 117.3) creates marginally better looks. At similar tempos, this is a toss-up that Iowa State edges on efficiency.

Duke's AdjEM (38.5) towers over Arizona's (27.5) by 11 points. Duke's offense (AdjO 130.6 vs 124.9) and defense (AdjD 92.0 vs 97.4) are both significantly better. Arizona's pace pushes the tempo, but Duke's efficiency holds at any speed.

Alabama's AdjEM (31.1) is 4.7 points above Wisconsin's (26.4). Wisconsin's slow tempo could limit Alabama's transition game, but Alabama's offensive efficiency (AdjO 127.7 vs 123.1) means they score effectively even in half-court sets.

Houston's AdjEM (37.3) is 11.1 points above Purdue's (26.2). Houston's historic defense (AdjD 87.3) is built to contain Purdue's elite offense (AdjO 125.6). Even at Purdue's pace, Houston's defense holds opponents to season lows.

Tennessee's AdjEM (28.3) edges Kentucky's (24.9) by 3.4 points, driven by elite defense (AdjD 91.9 vs 98.5). In a rivalry game, Tennessee's defensive identity and 6.6-point AdjD advantage is the deciding factor.
Florida's AdjEM (33.6) holds a 7.1-point edge over Maryland (26.5). Both have elite defenses (AdjD 94.2 vs 92.1), but Florida's offense (AdjO 127.8 vs 118.6) creates decisive separation.

St. John's AdjD (90.0) is the best in the bracket and 7.6 points better than Texas Tech's (97.6). Despite Texas Tech's higher AdjEM (28.5 vs 22.9), St. John's defense creates a rock-wall matchup problem. However, Texas Tech's offense (AdjO 126.1 vs 113.0) is the x-factor — St. John's needs their defense to dominate.

elite_8.reasoning: Auburn's AdjEM (34.7) is 8.7 points higher than Iowa State's (26.0). Both are strong defensive teams (AdjD 93.0 vs 93.3), but Auburn's offensive superiority (AdjO 127.7 vs 119.3) creates the separation. Auburn generates elite looks that Iowa State's defense can't fully contain.

Duke (AdjEM 38.5) holds a 7.4-point edge over Alabama (31.1). Both are offensive juggernauts (AdjO 130.6 vs 127.7), but Duke's defense (AdjD 92.0 vs 96.6) is the differentiator. In a high-scoring Elite Eight matchup, the better defensive team advances.

Two elite defensive teams — Houston's AdjD (87.3) vs Tennessee's (91.9). Houston's 9.0-point AdjEM gap (37.3 vs 28.3) comes from both offensive and defensive superiority. This becomes a historically low-scoring grinder that Houston's overall efficiency wins.

Florida's AdjEM (33.6) is 10.7 points above St. John's (22.9). St. John's elite defense (AdjD 90.0) will slow Florida, but Florida's offensive engine (AdjO 127.8 vs 113.0) generates enough firepower. The 14.8-point offensive gap is too wide for defense alone to close.

final_four.reasoning: Auburn (AdjEM 34.7) vs Florida (33.6) — a razor-thin 1.1-point edge for Auburn. Both are 1-seeds with elite offensive profiles (AdjO 127.7 vs 127.8), but Auburn's defense (AdjD 93.0 vs 94.2) is slightly better. In a Final Four coin-flip, Auburn's defensive edge is the tiebreaker.

Duke (AdjEM 38.5) vs Houston (37.3) — the two best teams in the country. Duke's generational offense (AdjO 130.6) is the x-factor against Houston's historic defense (AdjD 87.3). Duke has enough offensive firepower to crack Houston's wall.

championship.reasoning: Duke (AdjEM 38.5) vs Auburn (34.7) — a 3.8-point efficiency edge for Duke. Duke's offense (AdjO 130.6 vs 127.7) and defense (AdjD 92.0 vs 93.0) are both marginally better. In a championship game between two 1-seeds, Duke's complete statistical dominance gives them the edge.
